calendar.matters:
COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jul-20-2009 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Prove-Up Hearing. Comm: Willilam R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Lois Jones, CSR #3689.

Adam Anolik, Esq. and Jonathan Harriman, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Alexander Anolik a Professional Corporation, Trustee of the Arseny & Olga Kovshar Private Charitable Memorial Foundation.
Thomas Smallwood, Building Manager for plaintiff, was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant John Miller.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was filed on June 24, 2009.
Plaintiff's Trial Brief was filed today, July 20, 2009.

Exhibit 1--Office Lease dated July 17, 2007------------------------------------------ID/EVID.
Exhibit 2--3 Day Notice to Pay Rent or Quit dated June 19, 2009----------------------ID/EVID.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Alexander Anolik a Professional Corporation, Trustee of the Arseny & Olga Kovshar Private Charitable Memorial Foundation, and against defendant John Miller as follows:

Past-Due Rent - $6,186.66.
Holdover Damages - $746.66.
Attorney Fees - $5,500.00.
Costs - $215.00.
Total - $12,648.32.

Additionally, plaintiff Alexander Anolik a Professional Corporation, Trustee of the Arseny & Olga Kovshar Private Charitable Memorial Foundation is entitled to Restitution and Possession of the premises located at 2107 Van Ness Avenue, #306, San Francisco, CA 94103. The lease is forfeited.

Counsel for plaintiff shall submit a Proposed Judgment to the Court this afternoon.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-20-2009 Assigned To Trial Court

OTHER SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-16-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Judge Pro Tem: Barbara Anscher, Clerk: Felicia Green, Not Reported. (Dept. 218)

Appearances:
Tom Smallwood, property manager, appearing on behalf of plaintiff Alexander Anolik.
Attorney, Jonathan Harriman appearing for the plaintiff.

Defendant, John Miller is present.
Attorney, Dakota S. Whitney representing the defendant through limited scope.

This matter comes on calendar for an Unlawful Detainer Settlement Conference. The Judge Pro Tem, litigants and counsel confer privately for settlement discussions.

This case did not settle, the trial date remains set
